source.title: BSEE Accident Investigation Report - Dropped Object Incident

actions_taken: Added second secondary retention to opposite side of fingerboard
Installing NOV secondary retention safety wire and alternative latch system on trial basis
Discussion on following SSP for job being performed
event.summary: On January 23, 2022 at 0140 hours, a finger latch broke off and fell approximately 101 feet to the drill floor during drilling operations on the Diamond Offshore Black Lion. The Assistant Driller was racking back a stand of 6-5/8 inch drill pipe when it struck the finger latch. No injuries were reported. The incident was caused by non-compliance with Site-Specific Plan procedures, ineffective secondary retention, and an inoperable latch system due to faulty data transmission.
recommendations: Ensure drill crew follows Site-Specific Plan (SSP) prior to starting operations
Maintain flagger in derrick to confirm all intended fingers are fully open before racking pipe back
Ensure latch system is operational and providing accurate data to driller's monitor
Implement proper mitigations if SSP cannot be followed before proceeding with operation

hazards.name: Mechanical failure of finger latch system

threats.name: Finger latch struck by racking drill pipe
Finger latch system disabled due to faulty data
Inadequate personnel positioning for visual confirmation

consequences.name: Dropped finger latch falling 101 feet to drill floor

controls.name: Site-Specific Plan (SSP) for Trip Out of Hole
controls.barrier_role: Procedural control to ensure proper sequencing and personnel positioning
controls.lod_basis: Primary procedural control to prevent contact with closed finger latches

supporting_text: The investigation revealed that the drill crew did not follow procedure in reviewing the Site-Specific Plan (SSP) Trip Out of Hole
Diamond reviewed the incident with all the crews and discussed following the SSP for the job being performed

controls.name: Finger Watch positioned in derrick
controls.barrier_role: Visual confirmation that finger latches are in open position before racking pipe
controls.lod_basis: Direct visual inspection to prevent contact with closed latches

supporting_text: A Finger Watch was in place on the drill floor to look up and confirm that the finger latch was in the open position before the AD would rack a stand back in the derrick
The Finger Watch looked up and confirmed incorrectly that the fingers were open on the row where the stand was to be placed

Poor visibility due to nighttime operations and no clear view of individual latch fingers from his position on the rig floor hampered the finger watch's ability to determine if the finger latch was fully opened

controls.name: Latch system with remote status monitoring
controls.barrier_role: Automated system to provide latch status to driller and enable remote control
controls.lod_basis: Secondary control to provide real-time latch status and remote operation capability

supporting_text: The latch system was disabled due to inaccurate data transmitted back to the AD's monitor in the drillers shack
This system provided the status of each latch and the AD had the ability to open or close the latch when racking back stands in the derrick

controls.name: Secondary retention safety cable
controls.barrier_role: Catch and retain finger latch if it breaks free
controls.lod_basis: Final barrier to prevent dropped object from reaching lower levels

supporting_text: The 5-pound finger latch and secondary retention safety cable broke off with both falling approximately 101 feet to the drill floor

controls.name: Red Zone Management
controls.barrier_role: Prevent personnel exposure to dropped object hazard
controls.lod_basis: Administrative control to keep personnel out of impact zone

supporting_text: Red Zone Management was in place and no personnel were in the Red Zone where the finger latch landed

controls.name: Pre-tour safety meeting and Operational Work Order review
controls.barrier_role: Ensure crew awareness of hazards and procedures before operation
controls.lod_basis: Initial briefing to establish shared understanding of work procedures

supporting_text: A pre-tour Toolbox Talk was held and an Operational Work Order Details document about tripping pipe, was reviewed and signed by all personnel involved in the operation on the drill floor

controls.name: Mentor training for Finger Watch
controls.barrier_role: Ensure Finger Watch competency in identifying open finger latches
controls.lod_basis: Training to develop human capability for visual inspection task

supporting_text: The Finger Watch was shown by his assigned mentor what to look for when performing his job, as this was his first hitch offshore

controls.name: Stop Work Authority
controls.barrier_role: Halt operations and initiate investigation upon incident detection
controls.lod_basis: Immediate response to prevent escalation of incident consequences

supporting_text: A Stop Work Authority was utilized, and an investigation was initiated

notes.rules: JSON output only. mentioned fields must be evidence-based. Use null for unknown values.
